title: bridge.py: _is_l2_routing_payload is defined but never used
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/bridge.py
Lines: 377-382

The function _is_l2_routing_payload is defined but never called anywhere in the file:

```python
def _is_l2_routing_payload(payload: bytes) -> bool:
    return (
        len(payload) > 1
        and payload[0] == L2_DISPATCH_ROUTING
        and payload[1] == L2_ROUTING_TYPE_ANNOUNCE
    )
```

This is dead code. It imports L2_DISPATCH_ROUTING and L2_ROUTING_TYPE_ANNOUNCE from lichen.l2_payload but never uses them.

Impact: Unused code increases maintenance burden. Either this should be integrated into the RX path (to filter/handle routing messages specially), or removed.

Fix: Either:
1. Integrate into _on_link_rx() to handle L2 routing payloads appropriately
2. Or remove the function and the associated import
close_reason: Removed unused dead code and import per No Dead Code rule. Verified with ruff check (pre-existing issues only), pytest tests/interface/test_kiss_bridge.py (36 passed). Commit pushed.
